name: Acolyte's Journey
benefit1: You gain proficiency in light armor, shields and a single common group of weapons. This group may include Channeling Weapons, even though they aren't consider common weapons. (But you only get 1 of the possible channeling weapons).
benefit2: You gain +5 skill points in the Religion skill.
benefit3: You are welcome at establishments for your religion and they are obligated to help you with lodging, advice, food and sometimes equipment.

name: Advanced Artificing
benefit1: You gain Artificer Advanced Crafting Abilities
benefit2: You are able to craft artificers that cost 10000 clim or less.
benefit3: You gain +1 Burden that must be spent on an artifice.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Advanced Artificing
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
Artifice Modification B
Cost: X crafting points
Description: 
You add a B-Type modification to an artifice. See the Universal Artifice mods page as well as the individual weapon page for details. Each modification has its own cost.

Weapon Modification B
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You add an B-Type modification to the weapon. See the Universal Weapon page as well as the individual weapon page for details. Each modification has its own cost.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
Artifice Modification B
Cost: X crafting points
Description:
You add a B-Type modification to an artifice. See the Universal Artifice mods page as well as the individual weapon page for details. Each modification has its own cost.

Weapon Modification B
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description:
You add an B-Type modification to the weapon. See the Universal Weapon page as well as the individual weapon page for details. Each modification has its own cost.

name: Adventurer Essentials
benefit1: You gain proficiency in 1 common weapon group. In addition, you gain proficiency in 1 common or specialized weapon groups.
benefit2: You gain proficiency in 1 armor category of your choosing. Alternatively, you may pick the shield or greatshield.
benefit3: You may use Presence Concealment.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Presence Concealment
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: By suppressing your mana output you conceal your presence, gaining Presence Concealment.  You cannot use any abilites that cost mana or any abilities with the aura keyword while in Presence Concealment.  You can end this effect for 0 RP but the inability to use abilities that cost mana or abilities with the aura keyword persists until the end of your next turn.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: By suppressing your mana output you conceal your presence, gaining Presence Concealment.  You cannot use any abilites that cost mana or any abilities with the aura keyword while in Presence Concealment.  You can end this effect for 0 RP but the inability to use abilities that cost mana or abilities with the aura keyword persists until the end of your next turn.

name: Aerial Mage
benefit1: You may use Aerial Mode Switch.
benefit2: While grounded and riding an Aerial Stave, you may reload 2 shells instead of 1 when taking the Reload Artifice basic action on your staff. You may do this even if your hands would normally be full and can draw both shells with 1 action.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Aerial Mode Switch
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You switch between hover and aerial mode.

Hover mode allows you to attack with normal attacks with one hand. Your airships movement speed is reduced to 10ft while in hover mode. 

While in Aerial Mode you cannot use any attacks or actions that are not airship weapons or have the Aerial Mage keyword.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You switch between hover and aerial mode.

Hover mode allows you to attack with normal attacks with one hand. Your airships movement speed is reduced to 10ft while in hover mode.

While in Aerial Mode you cannot use any attacks or actions that are not airship weapons or have the Aerial Mage keyword.

name: Aftershocks
benefit1: You may use Aftershock.
benefit2: When using a Fighter, Highlander or Reaver ability that compares Power against a Boss type enemy, you may treat your Power as 2 higher for that purpose. (Does not affect the dmg roll etc)

associatedAbilityRef.name: Aftershock
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You attack with such force that you create aftershocks when you miss your attack, dealing Half Pierce Physical Damage equal to your Power to the target and all enemies within 5ft of it. 

If the target Dodged the triggering attack, this damage is reduced to half your Power instead.

If your attack targets multiple enemies, this only applies if you miss all enemies.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You attack with such force that you create aftershocks when you miss your attack, dealing Half Pierce Physical Damage equal to your Power to the target and all enemies within 5ft of it.

If the target Dodged the triggering attack, this damage is reduced to half your Power instead.

If your attack targets multiple enemies, this only applies if you miss all enemies.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementHtml: You missed a Heavy melee attack with a Two-Handed Melee Weapon.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementText: You missed a Heavy melee attack with a Two-Handed Melee Weapon.

name: Air Stability
benefit1: The cavalier’s Rider & Mount As One ability applies even while flying. Any damage you take while flying on a flying mount will cause you to lose altitude as if your mount took damage.

name: Angelic Presence
benefit1: You gain Angelic Presence, which gives a +1 to Presence.

name: Armorsmith Basics
benefit1: You gain proficiency in light armor, medium armor and shields.
benefit2: You gain proficiency in 1 basic melee weapon and can use a smith's hammer as a bludgeoning weapon in which you are proficient.
benefit3: You can craft armors, shields and cloaks using Iron, Tamahagane, Animal Hide and Direhide, as well as alloys. You may use an IP to perform an EP action tied to this key ability 4 times.
benefit4: You gain Armorsmith Core Crafting abilities.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Armorsmith Basics
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
Armorsmith's Modification A
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You add an A-Type modification to the armor. See the Universal Armor page as well as the individual armor pages for details. Each modification has its own cost.

Armorsmith's Modification Frame
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You add an Frame-Type modification to the armor. See the Universal Armor page as well as the individual armor pages for details. Each modification has its own cost.

Adaptive Layering
Cost: X crafting points
Description: 
You mix a special material into the armor or shield. Each material has its own crafting point cost.

Cloak Layering
Cost: X crafting points
Description: 
You use a special material for the creation of the cloak. Each material has its own crafting point cost. Only cloak special materials can be used and mixing in a special material increases the burden cost of the cloak by 1.

Create Ingot
Cost: Errand
Description: 
You create an ingot from armorsmithing base materials or from armorsmithing special materials.  See Item: Armorsmithing Materials.

Tan Leather
Cost: Errand
Description: 
You create a tanned hide from armorsmithing base leather materials. See item: Armorsmithing materials.

name: Armorsmithing Expert
benefit1: You gain the Master Armorer's core crafting abilities
benefit2: You may craft using Mithril, Orihalcum, Primehide and Dragonscale materials.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Armorsmithing Expert
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:

name: Arms Mastery
benefit1: You are considered proficient with every melee weapon you wield (This does not count as being proficient for class requirements).

name: Artificer Basics
benefit1: You gain Artificer Core Crafting Abilities
benefit2: You can craft using non-advanced materials and base materials. You may use an IP to perform an EP action tied to this key ability 4 times.
benefit3: You can now craft Artifices and Firearms. You are able to craft artifices that cost 2500 clim or less.
benefit4: You gain proficiency in the musket and the Artificer's Multitool.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Artificer Basics
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
Artifice Modification A
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You add an A-Type modification to an artifice. See the Universal Artifice mods page as well as the individual artifice page for details. Each modification has its own cost.

Weapon Modification A
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You add an A-Type modification to the weapon. See the Universal Weapon mods page as well as the individual weapon page for details. Each modification has its own cost.

Artifice Modification C
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You add an C-Type modification to the weapon. See the Universal Artifice mods page as well as the individual artifice page for details. Each modification has its own cost.

Craft Fuel Shells
Cost: Errand
Description: 
You craft up to 5 small shells.  You can instead craft a medium shell instead of 2 small shells.  Small shells require 50 magical fuel, medium shells require 200 magical fuel.

Craft Artifice Parts
Cost: Errand
Description: 
You create up to 3000u of artifice parts of the same type of blacksmithing material used to make them at a 1:1 rate. (3000u of Iron Creates 3000u of Iron Artifice Parts.)  See Items:  Blacksmithing Materials, Artificer Materials. When converting items of a higher tier, you can convert up to 6000 of tamahagane, 9000 of mithril, 12000 of Orihalcum or 15000 of escudo.

Firearm Alloy
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You mix a special material into the firearm or artifice weapon. Each material has its own crafting point cost.

name: Augmented Casting - Increased Reach
benefit1: You may use Increased Reach.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Increased Reach
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: The range of the next Spell you cast is doubled. Only works on Spells that have a range listed in ft.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: The range of the next Spell you cast is doubled. Only works on Spells that have a range listed in ft.

name: Augmented Casting - Persistent Spell
benefit1: You may use Persistent Spell.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Persistent Spell
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: If the next Spell you cast applies a buff or debuff, the duration of this buff or debuff is extended by 1 turn. If the Spell allows for a save, the target may make another save after the original effect is over to resist the duration extension.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: If the next Spell you cast applies a buff or debuff, the duration of this buff or debuff is extended by 1 turn. If the Spell allows for a save, the target may make another save after the original effect is over to resist the duration extension.

name: Authority
benefit1: You may treat any roll on Negotiation and Intimidation checks that are below 10 as being a 10 and may spend 2 mana before rolling to treat the roll as a 20 instead.
benefit2: One of your eyes becomes an Domination Eye. It may be used to make Mystic Eye type abilities of the appropriate type. An eye can only be a single mystic eye type.

name: Benevolence
benefit1: You gain Elemental Mastery: Holy
benefit2: Your attacks do not consume Illumination on miss.
benefit3: You may choose not to use the effect of Illumination on a target.(Must be declared before an attack is made).

name: Bone Armor
benefit1: You may use Bone Armor.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Bone Armor
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You target and destroy a corpse within range to give yourself Bone Armor, which gives you a +2 bonus to your guard. Bone Armor lasts until the end of the encounter or until you take 2 or more damage from an attack.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You target and destroy a corpse within range to give yourself Bone Armor, which gives you a +2 bonus to your guard. Bone Armor lasts until the end of the encounter or until you take 2 or more damage from an attack.

name: Botany Study
benefit1: You gain the ability to detect if foraged food is poisonous or unsafe to eat, nearly guaranteeing its safety.  If it is a common poison, you can also identify its effect.
benefit2: You gain the ability to properly process some poisonous mushrooms, fruits and herbs, making them edible as a Camp Action.  You can process up to 100u at a time.
benefit3: You gain the ability to harvest Tier 2 Foraging Outcrops.
benefit4: You gain proficiency in Artifice Chainsaws and may use it as a gathering tool.

name: Boundless Wind
benefit1: You may use Boundless Wind Stance.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Boundless Wind Stance
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You enter the Wind Stance, an unarmed style focusing around aerial combat. While in this stance, you may Hover and Fly up to your speed. If you already have a natural Flying speed or the Flight racial ability, your flying speed is increased  by 10ft.

This stance lasts until you enter a different stance or make an attack with anything other than a melee attack or an ability with the Unarmed Strike keyword.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You enter the Wind Stance, an unarmed style focusing around aerial combat. While in this stance, you may Hover and Fly up to your speed. If you already have a natural Flying speed or the Flight racial ability, your flying speed is increased  by 10ft.

This stance lasts until you enter a different stance or make an attack with anything other than a melee attack or an ability with the Unarmed Strike keyword.

name: Brace
benefit1: When using the Prepare action to prepare a melee attack with a polearm, if your target of that prepared attack moved more than 20ft that turn (excluding teleports), your attack gains Full Pierce and does maximum damage. Against a mounted enemy this also gains Sure Hit and on damage, their movement becomes 0 until the start of your turn.
benefit2: On mastering this class, the Sure Hit property (but not the movement nullification) of your prepared attack against mounted enemies now also applies against Beast type enemies that are large size or bigger.

name: Cannon Expert
benefit1: You gain proficiency in Cannons.
benefit2: Your attacks with the Cannon keyword ignore the 1 attack limitation of cannons.
benefit3: You may use Rocket Jump.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Rocket Jump
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You fire your cannon behind you and use the momentum to sidestep 10ft. This movement does not reduce the range of your cannon.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You fire your cannon behind you and use the momentum to sidestep 10ft. This movement does not reduce the range of your cannon.

name: Carpenter Basics
benefit1: You can use a carpenters chisel as a crafting tool for carpenters crafts and buildings.
benefit2: You can craft using Pine and Maple, as well as alloys. You may use an IP to perform an EP action tied to this key ability 4 times.
benefit3: You gain proficiency in axes. You also gain proficiency in either bows or crossbows.
benefit4: You can use the Carpenter's core crafting abilities.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Carpenter Basics
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
Carpenter's Modification A
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You add an A-Type modification to the weapon or tool. See the Universal Weapon page as well as the individual weapon page for details. Each modification has its own cost.

Joining and Assembling
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You mix a special material into the carpentry project. Each material has its own crafting point cost.

Create Oil Coating
Cost: Errand
Description:
You create an Oil Coating from Carpenter materials.  See Items: Carpenter Materials.

Create Wood Block
Cost: Errand
Description:
You create a Wood Block from Carpenter materials.  See Items: Carpenter Materials.

Infuse Wand
Cost: 5 Crafting Points
Description: 
You add a drop of Oil Coating into a wand, changing the built in damage type to the element of the oil. Only works with oils that change the damage type. Does not consume the oil.

name: Celestial Artillery
benefit1: When attacking an enemy that’s further than 80ft away, your spells with the Celestial keyword gain the Artillery keyword.
benefit2: You may use Comet.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Comet
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You make a Light (1 AP) or Heavy (2 AP) attack against the target that deals Arcane damage.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You make a Light (1 AP) or Heavy (2 AP) attack against the target that deals Arcane damage.

name: Cloudstep
benefit1: You may use Cloudstep.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Cloudstep
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You may Sidestep up to half your speed. This movement ignores difficult terrain.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You may Sidestep up to half your speed. This movement ignores difficult terrain.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementHtml: Must be the first movement action in your turn.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementText: Must be the first movement action in your turn.

name: Controlled Blaze
benefit1: When you deal Fire Damage, you may choose not to ignite flammable objects.
benefit2: You gain Fire Mastery.

name: Controlled Opportunities
benefit1: You gain Heavy Armor proficiency.
benefit2: When making an attack of opportunity, you gain a +2 bonus to the accuracy and damage roll.

name: Controlled Wind
benefit1: When pushing a target back or up with a Wind Spell, you may choose to push up to the maximum distance instead of always pushing the maximum distance.

name: Cool Spotlight
benefit1: Your Idol songs gain +15ft range.  This stacks with other key abilities that increase their range.
benefit2: Your One Beat, One Heart song does not count towards your song limit.
benefit3: When paying upkeep for One Beat, One Heart, you may spend 1 mana to double its effect.

name: Craft Weapons
benefit1: You gain Blacksmith Core Crafting Abilities.
benefit2: You can craft using the Iron and Tamahagane base materials, as well as any alloys. You may use an IP to perform an EP action tied to this key ability 4 times.
benefit3: You can use a Smith’s Hammer as a crafting tool for blacksmithing crafts. You may also use it as a Bludgeoning weapon in which you are proficient.
benefit4: You gain proficiency in 1 smithing weapon.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Blacksmith Core Crafting
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
Blacksmith’s Modification A
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You add an A-Type modification to the weapon or tool. See the Universal Weapon or Crafting mods page as well as the individual weapon page for details. Each modification has its own cost.

Weapon Alloy
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You mix a special material into the smithing weapon. Each material has its own crafting point cost.

Create Ingot

Description:
You create an ingot from blacksmithing base materials or from blacksmithing special materials.  See Item: Blacksmithing Materials.

name: Culinary Basics
benefit1: You gain Culinarian Core Crafting Abilities
benefit2: You can craft stocks using advanced and base materials. You may use an IP to perform an EP action tied to this key ability 4 times.
benefit3: You can use a Frying Pan as a crafting tool for culinarian crafts. You may also use it as a Light Bludgeoning weapon in which you are proficient.
benefit4: You gain proficiency in small weapons.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Culinary Basics
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
Mushroom Stock
Cost: 15 Crafting Points
Description: 
Turns a Gourmet Base Stock into a Mushroom Stock (does not stack with other stocks), which when added to a cooked meal gives a +1 Focus Food Bonus that lasts for 24 hours. Requires 1 Box of Fresh Mushrooms. 

Meaty Stock
Cost: 15 Crafting Points
Description: 
Turns a Gourmet Base Stock into a Meaty Stock (does not stack with other stocks), which when added to a cooked meal gives a +1 Power Food Bonus that lasts for 24 hours.  Requires 1 Box of Fresh Meat.

Fish Stock
Cost: 15 Crafting Points
Description: 
Turns a Gourmet Base Stock into a Fish Stock (does not stack with other stocks), which when added to a cooked meal gives a +1 Toughness Food Bonus that lasts for 24 hours.  Requires 1 Box of Fresh Fish.

Vegetable Stock
Cost: 15 Crafting Points
Description: 
Turns a Gourmet Base Stock into a Vegetable Stock (does not stack with other stocks), which when added to a cooked meal gives a +1 Agility Food Bonus that lasts for 24 hours.  Requires 1 Box of Fresh Vegetables.

Spicy Stock
Cost: 30 Crafting Points
Description: 
Turns a Gourmet Base Stock into a Spicy Stock (does not stack with other stocks), which when added to a cooked meal gives a +1 Crafting Food Bonus that lasts for 24 hours.  Requires 1 bundle of Spicy Peppers.

Herbal Stock
Cost: 30 Crafting Points
Description: 
Turns a Gourmet Base Stock into a Herbal Stock (does not stack with other stocks), which when added to a cooked meal gives a +1 Gathering Food Bonus that lasts for 24 hours. Requires 1 bundle of Fresh Cooking Herbs. When gathering from a node with a bonus from this stock, you keep the benefit for that entire node unless you go a day without harvesting from that node.

Stir Layered Flavors
Cost: 30 Crafting Points
Description: 
Allows you to add another flavor, allowing you to add a second different flavor.  Choose from Mushroom, Meaty, Fish, Vegetable, Spicy or Herbal—then add the corresponding ingredient.  For example:  A Mushroom stock + Meaty stock becomes a Mushroom-Meaty stock that grants +1 Power / +1 Focus Food Bonus.  

Use Every Last Drop
Cost: 15 Crafting Points
Description:  
Increases the stock yield by 2.

Secret Ingredient
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You mix a special material into the stock.  Each material has its own crafting point cost.

Create Box or Bundle of Food
Cost: Errand.
Description: 
You create a perfect mix of ingredients that would be worthy of a stock with 500u of matching material.  Example:  500u of Meat can make 1 Box of Fresh Meat.  You can make 5 boxes of any kind for one errand action.

name: Cultivator's Journey
benefit1: Your unarmed attack damage is treated as a One-Handed Weapon in which you are proficient.
benefit2: You gain proficiency in light armor, light swords and another weapon of your choice.
benefit3: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Appraise, Athletics, Deception, Perception, Insight, Medicine, Intimidation, Stealth or Common Knowledge. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.

name: Dark Surge
benefit1: After you deal damage to yourself, you gain Dark Surge for your next unarmed attack that turn. Dark Surge gives you +1 Accuracy.

name: Death Perception
benefit1: You may use Death Perception.
benefit2: One of your eyes becomes a Shinigami Eye. It may be used to make Mystic Eye type abilities of the appropriate type. An eye can only be a single mystic eye type.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Death Perception
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You can assess the approximate HP percentage of all living units within range until the end of combat. (The DM should enable health bars for the unit). Only affects unit in your line of sight when you use this ability, if more enemies join the fight later they are not affected until you use this ability again.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You can assess the approximate HP percentage of all living units within range until the end of combat. (The DM should enable health bars for the unit). Only affects unit in your line of sight when you use this ability, if more enemies join the fight later they are not affected until you use this ability again.

name: Deflect Everything
benefit1: You may use your Parry ability against every kind of attack, as well as against Save effects that you could Block.

name: Dimensional Armory
benefit1: You can create a pocket dimension with Dimensional Armory.  Creating the pocket dimension and establishing its door takes 1 week and costs 1 errand point.  The entry point can be any regular door that you fit through.  When the creator opens this door, they can decide instead to access the Dimensional Armory, which is a 15ft x 15ft closet.  This closet can hold up to 6 Burden worth of items.  Some magic items can be rejected by the dimensional armory (GM’s discretion), such as Divine Arms not bonded to you.  If the owner of a Dimensional Armory dies, everything inside of it appears in the next unoccupied space possible next to the owner.
benefit2: Any burden reducing items (such as an alchemy rig) in the armory have no effect.

name: Disable Magic
benefit1: When using Decipher Magic, you are able to disable both tier 1 and tier 2 level bounded fields.
benefit2: You are able to disable or change who is excluded from your own Bounded Fields for 0 RP.

name: Discern Immunities
benefit1: You may use Discern Immunities.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Discern Immunities
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You look at a single target and glean what its resistances are until the end of the encounter. When you are about to use an ability with a negative effect that would be passively negated by the target, you become aware of this and may cancel your action.

Does not apply if the target has an active ability that can remove it, such as a paladin’s Paragon of Virtue for example.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You look at a single target and glean what its resistances are until the end of the encounter. When you are about to use an ability with a negative effect that would be passively negated by the target, you become aware of this and may cancel your action.

Does not apply if the target has an active ability that can remove it, such as a paladin’s Paragon of Virtue for example.

name: Draw Elements
benefit1: You may use Draw Elements.

name: Draw Elements II
benefit1: You may use Draw Elements II.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Draw Elements II
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You may choose an element. Until the start of your next turn, you are considered to have mastered that element.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You may choose an element. Until the start of your next turn, you are considered to have mastered that element.

name: Earth Weapon
benefit1: When attacking with a weapon, you may choose to have the weapon deal Earth Damage instead of its normal damage type. You must declare you chosen damage type before making the accuracy roll.

name: Elemental Convergence
benefit1: You may use Elemental Convergence.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Elemental Convergence
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You may choose 1 element from Arcane, Fire, Water, Frost, Wind, Lightning or Earth. Until the end of the encounter, you are considered to have mastered that element.

You may swap this element once per turn for 1 Mana.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You may choose 1 element from Arcane, Fire, Water, Frost, Wind, Lightning or Earth. Until the end of the encounter, you are considered to have mastered that element.

You may swap this element once per turn for 1 Mana.

name: Emergency Shields
benefit1: You may cast Power Word: Shield and Power Word: Guardian Shield (when obtained) by spending 1 RP instead of 2 AP.

name: Empty Body
benefit1: You may use Empty Body.

name: Envenom Weapon
benefit1: You may use Envenom Weapon.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Envenom Weapon
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You enchant your weapon with a minor venom, making your attacks deal 1 additional full pierce Poison damage when you hit an enemy.

Lasts until the end of the encounter.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You enchant your weapon with a minor venom, making your attacks deal 1 additional full pierce Poison damage when you hit an enemy.

Lasts until the end of the encounter.

name: Eternal Flames
benefit1: When you would apply Burning on your turn, you instead apply Hellfire. Hellfire functions like Burning, but it can no longer be removed the normal way if not removed before the end of the target’s next turn. Hellfire can be applied to enemies immune to Burning, but it can still be removed like normal even after a turn has passed. Hellfire is automatically removed at the end of the encounter.
benefit2: If you apply Hellfire to a target that is already burning, you deal extra fire damage equal to your Power.

name: Eternal Mirane Cage
benefit1: You may use Eternal Mirane Cage.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Eternal Mirane Cage
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: Instead of killing the target, they are reduced to negative their Max HP +1. The target is encased in Magical Ice for 10 years, rooted on the location and cannot be moved.  The Ice never melts despite temperature and can only be dispelled by Higher Order Magic (such as a Divine Arms), or by you. The Spell effect ends if you die. You can extend the duration of the Frost by 10 years for every 2 mana spent.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: Instead of killing the target, they are reduced to negative their Max HP +1. The target is encased in Magical Ice for 10 years, rooted on the location and cannot be moved.  The Ice never melts despite temperature and can only be dispelled by Higher Order Magic (such as a Divine Arms), or by you. The Spell effect ends if you die. You can extend the duration of the Frost by 10 years for every 2 mana spent.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementHtml: Use when an ally attack would kill a target and target is not an ally.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementText: Use when an ally attack would kill a target and target is not an ally.

name: Evil Eye
benefit1: While you have a Mystic Eye active, you gain a +10 Mystic Eye bonus to perception checks relying on sight.
benefit2: One of your eyes becomes an Evil Eye. It may be used to make Mystic Eye type abilities of the appropriate type. An eye can only be a single mystic eye type.

name: Execute the Heretic
benefit1: You may use Execute Heretic

associatedAbilityRef.name: Execute Heretic
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You deal damage to the target equal to their max HP.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You deal damage to the target equal to their max HP.

name: Expanded Circuits
benefit1: You gain Expanded Circuits, which gives +2 maximum mana.

name: Expanded Menagerie
benefit1: You may spend 1 mana with using Summon Animal to choose which animal you summon.
benefit2: You add the Cheetah and Landshark to the pool of animals you can summon.

name: Expert Alchemist
benefit1: You gain Alchemeister Core Crafting Abilities.
benefit2: When using Throw Potion, you may infuse it using your own mana. You spend X mana and the mana cost the target needs to pay to benefit from the potion is reduced by X (the mana cost cannot go below 0).

associatedAbilityRef.name: Alchemeister Core Crafting
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
Masterful Dilution
Cost: 40 Crafting Points
Description: 
By diluting the contents to the perfect required amounts, you gain an additional Elixir, Potion, Flask, Potion or Salve upon crafting success. Stacks with Efficient Dilution.

Distillation
Cost: 40 Crafting Points
Description: 
You extract the highest quality healing materials from herbs, granting Healing +8 to a Potion with a heal or shielding effect.

Explosive Lining
Cost: 10 Crafting Points
Description: 
You carefully add an alchemical compound to a pressurized Flask. If the Flask has an area of effect, increases the area by another 5ft (example a 10ft radius becomes 15ft radius).

Purify Elixir
Cost: 40 Crafting Points
Description: 
You reduce the toxicity of an Elixir that has been detoxified while still maintaining its effect, reducing the damage from taking an Elixir by an additional 5.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:

Purify Elixir

name: Expert Farmer
benefit1: You may use the Agrarian's core crafting abilities.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Expert Farmer
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
Greater Infuse Qi
Keywords: Rapid
Description: 
You infuse one of the planted herbs with Qi, upgrading it from rare to mystic and adding 20 to its difficulty.

Bountiful Harvest II
Keywords: Rapid
Description: 
You bless one of the planted seeds that grants Food units or Alchemy materials. It grants 4 times that amount and you add 10 to its difficulty. Does not stack.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
Greater Infuse Qi
Keywords: Rapid
Description:
You infuse one of the planted herbs with Qi, upgrading it from rare to mystic and adding 20 to its difficulty.

Bountiful Harvest II
Keywords: Rapid
Description:
You bless one of the planted seeds that grants Food units or Alchemy materials. It grants 4 times that amount and you add 10 to its difficulty. Does not stack.

name: Expert Scribe
benefit1: You gain +5 points in Art: Calligraphy expertise skill. This is not doubled for expertise bonus.
benefit2: You may draw scrolls, talismans or similar objects for 0 AP once per turn. When you do, reduce the AP cost of that talisman by 1. Even if you do not consume the talisman, it only benefits from the AP reduction once per turn.

name: Faerie Light Eyes
benefit1: Faerie Light Eyes provide protection against Illusions, giving you the ability to immediately see the reality warping distortions that illusions cause.  The user is not only notified of illusions, but immune to all but your own.
benefit2: One of your eyes becomes a Faerie Light Eye. It may be used to make Mystic Eye type abilities of the appropriate type. An eye can only be a single mystic eye type.

name: Fallen Friend
benefit1: When an Heroic tier ally reaches 0 HP, you may immediately enter Rage Stance for 0 AP and RP as well as 0 mana. You can use this even if you don’t have the Rage ability yet.

name: Fancy Footwork
benefit1: You gain proficiency with Dueling Weapons. While wielding a dueling weapon, you also gain the light blades' Nimble ability, even if not wielding or proficient with light blades.

name: Farming Basics
benefit1: You gain proficiency in the scythe specialized weapon and can use it as farming tool. You also gain proficiency in the Sickle and can use it as a farming tool and One-Handed axe.
benefit2: You can plant seeds in plots of land that sprout into harvestable Food and Alchemy materials. You can work 10 plots of land.
benefit3: You gain Farmer core abilities.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Farming Basics
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
Spiritual Ground
Cost: 1 Interlude Point
Description: 
You infuse the land with your spiritual energy, creating up to 10 plots of land suitable for farming. These plots of lands can grow food and herbs at an accelerated rate and allow the planting of tier 1 rare herbs.

All plots of land must be filled with seeds before they begin growing. 

After creating a spiritual ground, you may immediately use Sow Seeds in it for 0 IP.

You can only have 1 spiritual ground at the same time.

Sow Seeds
Cost: 1 Interlude Point
Description: 
You may plant seeds in plots of land. You may plant 1 set of seeds in each plot of land you can work.

Harvest
Cost: 1 Interlude Point
Description: 
You harvest the planted seeds or take care of the plants not ready to be harvested yet. Do a crafting session. Your crafting score is the total value of seeds you can harvest or take care of.

If your crafting score is not high enough to harvest or take care of all seeds you planted, you must give up on some of the seeds. Starting from the seed with the highest difficulty, you drop seeds until your total crafting score matches or exceeds the total difficulty of the seeds.

Infuse Qi
Keywords: Rapid
Description: 
You infuse one of the planted herbs with Qi, upgrading it from common to rare and adding 10 to its difficulty.

Bountiful Harvest
Keywords: Rapid
Description: 
You bless one of the planted seeds that grants Food units or Alchemy materials. It grants double that amount and you add 5 to its difficulty. Does not stack.

name: Fighter's Journey
benefit1: You gain proficiency in 5 common weapons groups.
benefit2: You gain proficiency in all light armor, medium armor and normal shields.
benefit3: You gain +5 HP.

name: Fire Stance
benefit1: You may use Tempestuous Fire Stance

associatedAbilityRef.name: Tempestuous Fire Stance
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You enter the Tempestuous Fire Stance. While in this Stance, before you make an attack with the Unarmed Strike keyword, you may choose to move 5ft per AP spent on the move. This movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity. This Stance lasts until you enter a different stance or until you move, with the exceptions of this ability and movement abilities with the Unarmed Strike keyword. Making an attack does not cancel this stance.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You enter the Tempestuous Fire Stance. While in this Stance, before you make an attack with the Unarmed Strike keyword, you may choose to move 5ft per AP spent on the move. This movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity. This Stance lasts until you enter a different stance or until you move, with the exceptions of this ability and movement abilities with the Unarmed Strike keyword. Making an attack does not cancel this stance.

name: Fire Weapon
benefit1: When attacking with a weapon, you may choose to have the weapon deal Fire Damage instead of its normal damage type. You must declare your chosen damage type before making the accuracy roll.

name: Five Harmonies
benefit1: You may use Luminous Form Stance.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Luminous Form Stance
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You enter the Luminous Form Stance and create the Threads of Light. These manifest spiritual cloth, spectral arms or something similar that enhances your reach.

While in this stance, you gain an additional 10ft range on your unarmed attacks and your threatened range becomes 5-15ft. Whenever you deal damage to an enemy with an unarmed strike ability (not basic attacks), you gain 1 stack of Harmony up to a maximum of 5. You cannot gain more than 1 Harmony per use of ability.

When you leave this stance all stacks are lost.

This stance lasts until you enter a different stance or you willingly move, with the exception of movement abilities with the unarmed strike keyword. Abilities from allies that allow you to move, such as Gravitional Ki, count as willing movement.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You enter the Luminous Form Stance and create the Threads of Light. These manifest spiritual cloth, spectral arms or something similar that enhances your reach.

While in this stance, you gain an additional 10ft range on your unarmed attacks and your threatened range becomes 5-15ft. Whenever you deal damage to an enemy with an unarmed strike ability (not basic attacks), you gain 1 stack of Harmony up to a maximum of 5. You cannot gain more than 1 Harmony per use of ability.

When you leave this stance all stacks are lost.

This stance lasts until you enter a different stance or you willingly move, with the exception of movement abilities with the unarmed strike keyword. Abilities from allies that allow you to move, such as Gravitional Ki, count as willing movement.

name: Focused Weapons
benefit1: You gain Forgemaster Core Crafting Abilities.
benefit2: You can craft using Mithril and Orihalcum.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Forgemaster Core Crafting
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
Blacksmith’s Modification B
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description: 
You add an B-Type modification to the weapon or tool. See the Universal Weapon or Crafting mods page as well as the individual weapon page for details. Each modification has its own cost.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
Blacksmith’s Modification B
Cost: X Crafting Points
Description:
You add an B-Type modification to the weapon or tool. See the Universal Weapon or Crafting mods page as well as the individual weapon page for details. Each modification has its own cost.

name: Forager's Path
benefit1: You gain proficiency in Giant Scissors and proficiency in your choice between Axes or Hori. You may use axes for harvesting foraging outcrops.
benefit2: You gain the ability to harvest Tier 1 Foraging Outcrops.
benefit3: You gain the ability to use natural item as replacements for an Adventurers Kit and Torches such as:  using the sun/moss as a compass, using vines as rope etc.  The material must be available (GM’s discretion)
benefit4: You gain the ability to create Wood Blocks as an Errand.  See item: Carpenter Materials. You may use an IP to do this 4 times instead.

name: Frost Weapon
benefit1: When attacking with a weapon, you may choose to have the weapon deal Frost Damage instead of its normal damage type. You must declare you chosen damage type before making the accuracy roll.

name: Fungal Metabolite
benefit1: Due to symbiotic fungi within your body, you become resistant to all commonly known (book) poisons and may use Resist Poison.
benefit2: After being infected by an exotic poison or disease and surviving, that poison/disease is added to your list of resistances. Any disease or poison that you are resistant to can be used with Amoxicillin.
benefit3: Against a poison you are resistant to, you may use Resist Poison for 0 RP, 1 Mana instead of 1 RP.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Resist Poison
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You gain Resist Poison, which gives you Damage Reduction 100 against the triggering damage. You also resist the application of the poison, negating its effect.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You gain Resist Poison, which gives you Damage Reduction 100 against the triggering damage. You also resist the application of the poison, negating its effect.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementHtml: You're about to take Poison damage or become afflicted with a Poison.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementText: You're about to take Poison damage or become afflicted with a Poison.

name: Giant Strength
benefit1: While in Rage Stance, you may choose to have your size be treated as one size bigger than it actually is. You get an additional +5 bonus to Fitness checks during Rage stance regardless.

name: Gravity Master
benefit1: When you push or pull an enemy on your turn, you may spend 1 mana to increase this distance by 10ft. You may also spend 1 mana to instead ignore the effects of 1 source of Immovable on a target you’re attempting to push, unless that target is permanently Immovable.

name: Greatshield Master
benefit1: You gain proficiency in Greatshields. While wearing a Greatshield, any ally standing behind you that is the target of a ranged attack may treat you as high cover.

name: Gun Martial Arts
benefit1: You can fire your pistols while in melee range of an enemy without provoking attacks of opportunity.
benefit2: You can make unarmed attacks even if carrying a pistol in both hands. If wielding a weapon other than a pistol, this no longer applies. When wielding only pistols and no other weapons, your unarmed attacks benefit from your pistol material bonuses.

name: Gunslinger's Agility
benefit1: You gain proficiency in pistols.
benefit2: You gain +1 Gunslinger bonus to Initiative.

name: Heavy Cavalry
benefit1: You gain Medium Armor and Heavy Armor proficiency.
benefit2: Every enemy damaged by your Trample effects become Challenged by you until the start of your next turn.

name: Holy Orders
benefit1: You may use Holy Orders.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Holy Orders
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You target an enemy unit within range, causing the target to become your Holy Order until the start of your next turn. 

While active, only your Holy Order may be chosen as the target for any offensive ability, or must be included as a target to use an offensive action. 

If you make an attack of opportunity against your Holy Order, or if they move away from you while in your melee range, you can Sidestep up to twice your Speed towards them for 0 RP after their movement finishes.

Can only be used on your turn before you use an offensive action.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You target an enemy unit within range, causing the target to become your Holy Order until the start of your next turn.

While active, only your Holy Order may be chosen as the target for any offensive ability, or must be included as a target to use an offensive action.

If you make an attack of opportunity against your Holy Order, or if they move away from you while in your melee range, you can Sidestep up to twice your Speed towards them for 0 RP after their movement finishes.

Can only be used on your turn before you use an offensive action.

name: Holy Weapon
benefit1: When attacking with a weapon, you may choose to have the weapon deal Holy Damage instead of its normal damage type.
benefit2: You may choose to have your weapon emit light as if it was a lantern. You can enable or disable this effect for 1 AP.

name: Iaijutsu
benefit1: If you become the target of an attack while you're surprised, you may still react to it and any special properties of an attack that require you to be unaware of the attack (such as Killshot) do not apply to it.
benefit2: You may sheathe and draw katanas for 0 AP once per turn per katana.
benefit3: You start each battle in the 'Iai' state, which is used for Iai abilities. Making an attack with a katana removes the Iai state for that weapon. Using a skill that requires the Iai state requires the weapon to be sheathed and will remove the Iai state for ALL your weapons, unless specified otherwise.

name: Ice Cold
benefit1: You are immune to Slows and difficult terrain effects with the Frost keyword.
benefit2: You are immune to regular weather effects involving cold.
benefit3: You gain Frost Mastery.  Mastery of an element allows you to use it in Channeling weapon attacks and use it to perform minor tricks relating to it.

name: Idol's Journey
benefit1: You gain proficiency in wands, small weapons and light armor.
benefit2: You gain +5 skill points in Art.
benefit3: You may use One Beat, One Heart

associatedAbilityRef.name: One Beat, One Heart
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
All allies within range gain a +1 Song bonus to their accuracy. This bonus is doubled on Precise attacks.

Upkeep: 1 AP
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
All allies within range gain a +1 Song bonus to their accuracy. This bonus is doubled on Precise attacks.

Upkeep: 1 AP

name: Improved Death Perception
benefit1: You can see the death of non-living things allowing you to automatically do maximum, full pierce damage against objects.
benefit2: You apply 1 Mark of Death every time you hit an enemy with ‘Lines of Death’ or ‘Point of Death’ up to a maximum of 7 marks.

name: Improvised Weaponry
benefit1: When using improvised weapons, they deal the same damage as normal weapons would.
benefit2: You may use Chair Grab.
benefit3: Upon mastering this class, your improvised weapon attacks all gain the Prone keyword.

name: Inconspicuous
benefit1: If you're traveling stealthily, you may roll separate stealth checks even if traveling with the party. When doing this, any surprise rounds for you are determined purely off your own rolls and not your parties.
benefit2: During a surprise round, enemies you attack are still treated as having 0 RP for the purposes of ability requirements even after you attack them until they start their turn. You still gain this benefit, even if your allies attacked them already.

name: Independent Actor
benefit1: When working alone (No known allies within 300ft of you) you gain a +10 bonus to Perception, Insight, Survival and Stealth checks. Must be alone for at least 15 minutes to gain this benefit.
benefit2: You gain a +5 bonus in Art (Cartography) expertise. This is not doubled for expertise bonus.
benefit3: You may use Scout’s Retreat.
benefit4: Having an Aetherie or similar connection active automatically fails the ‘No known allies’ requirements.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Scout's Retreat
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You disengage and may move up to two times your speed away from enemies towards the closest escape point.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You disengage and may move up to two times your speed away from enemies towards the closest escape point.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementText: No known allies within 300ft of you

name: Infiltrate
benefit1: When you use Decipher Magic, you may use Roguecraft instead of Magic as the skill for it. In addition, you can modify the effect in such a way that it no longer affects you and your allies rather than completely disabling it.

name: Jump
benefit1: When using an ability with the Jump keyword, you do not take fall damage.
benefit2: You may use Jump.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Jump
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You leap up to 30ft into the air and then Sidestep up to twice your Speed. You may use Skydive at any point during this movement.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You leap up to 30ft into the air and then Sidestep up to twice your Speed. You may use Skydive at any point during this movement.

name: Knight of Yddrasil
benefit1: You may use Fluttering Blade.
benefit2: If an enemy is holding a weapon affected by your Fluttering Blade, you may spend 1 RP to deal Heavy Full Pierce damage to it.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Fluttering Blade
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You infuse a melee weapon that you are familiar with (belongs to you and was originally in your combat inventory at the start of the encounter) with magic, causing it to float into the air that listens to your directions.  You can have one Fluttering Blade at a time. 

When a Fluttering Blade weapon is hit by an attack, it drops to the ground and becomes inert.

Fluttering Blade has a base evasion of 7+Your Agility. Instead of moving, you can instead direct your Fluttering Blade to move, it has a base movement speed of 30ft. You can also direct your Fluttering Blade to attack within range. It can make basic Heavy, Light and Precise attacks as well as special Fae Knight attacks.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You infuse a melee weapon that you are familiar with (belongs to you and was originally in your combat inventory at the start of the encounter) with magic, causing it to float into the air that listens to your directions.  You can have one Fluttering Blade at a time.

When a Fluttering Blade weapon is hit by an attack, it drops to the ground and becomes inert.

Fluttering Blade has a base evasion of 7+Your Agility. Instead of moving, you can instead direct your Fluttering Blade to move, it has a base movement speed of 30ft. You can also direct your Fluttering Blade to attack within range. It can make basic Heavy, Light and Precise attacks as well as special Fae Knight attacks.

name: Knight of Yddrasil II
benefit1: You may use Fae Knight Style: Willow Stance

associatedAbilityRef.name: Fae Knight Style: Willow Stance
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You enter Fae Knight Style: Willow Stance. Increasing your Fluttering Blade Limit to 2. Shields can be used as Fluttering Blades while in Willow Stance. 

For 1 mana, all Fluttering Blades created in Willow Stance this turn can be enhanced with Heavy Spin, allowing them to be used for some Willow abilities but they no longer gain your focus as extra evasion.  Heavy Spin blades also deal 5d6 upgraded heavy attack damage, even if they were originally a one-handed weapon.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You enter Fae Knight Style: Willow Stance. Increasing your Fluttering Blade Limit to 2. Shields can be used as Fluttering Blades while in Willow Stance.

For 1 mana, all Fluttering Blades created in Willow Stance this turn can be enhanced with Heavy Spin, allowing them to be used for some Willow abilities but they no longer gain your focus as extra evasion.  Heavy Spin blades also deal 5d6 upgraded heavy attack damage, even if they were originally a one-handed weapon.

name: Light Cavalry
benefit1: While mounted and while you and your mount both wear light armor or no armor, you ignore natural difficult terrain.

name: Lightning Strikes Twice
benefit1: When the Static you apply deals damage to an enemy, that enemy becomes afflicted with Static X, where X is the value of the Static that damaged it.

name: Lightning Weapon
benefit1: When attacking with a weapon, you may choose to have the weapon deal Lightning Damage instead of its normal damage type. You must declare your chosen damage type before making the accuracy roll.

name: Living Cover
benefit1: While mounted, any ally within 5ft of you may use you as Low Cover. If an attack would miss them because of your cover benefit, the attack instead hits you.

name: Look of Death
benefit1: You may use Perish.
benefit2: One of your eyes becomes an Destruction Eye. It may be used to make Mystic Eye type abilities of the appropriate type. An eye can only be a single mystic eye type.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Perish
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You make a Light (1 AP), Heavy (2 AP) or Precise (2 AP) attack against the target that deals Dark(Curse).
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You make a Light (1 AP), Heavy (2 AP) or Precise (2 AP) attack against the target that deals Dark(Curse).

name: Lord's Dominion
benefit1: You can partially resist the effect of the sun. You may now spend 1 round in the sun before you start taking damage. Spending a turn outside of the sun resets this effect, giving you another round where you do not suffer the negative effects.
benefit2: You gain +1 Vampire Lord bonus to Presence.

name: Mage Armor
benefit1: You may use Mage Armor

associatedAbilityRef.name: Mage Armor
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You gain magical Light Armor, Medium Armor or Heavy Armor until the end of the encounter. This function just as normal armor of that type would, giving the same benefits and penalties. Does not stack with other armor, apply the effects of the Mage Armor This magical armor does not require proficiency of any sort and does not cost any Burden to wear and carry.

You may apply the effects of Barrier, Fortress, Manaweave, Protective Coating or Spidersilk to this armor (It must still meet the requirements, you cannot put fortress on a light armor for example). This can only be done when you first apply it, not if you later change your armor during combat.

If Mage Armor is active, you may spend 2 AP to change its type or to remove it.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You gain magical Light Armor, Medium Armor or Heavy Armor until the end of the encounter. This function just as normal armor of that type would, giving the same benefits and penalties. Does not stack with other armor, apply the effects of the Mage Armor This magical armor does not require proficiency of any sort and does not cost any Burden to wear and carry.

You may apply the effects of Barrier, Fortress, Manaweave, Protective Coating or Spidersilk to this armor (It must still meet the requirements, you cannot put fortress on a light armor for example). This can only be done when you first apply it, not if you later change your armor during combat.

If Mage Armor is active, you may spend 2 AP to change its type or to remove it.

name: Mage Knight
benefit1: You may also use Canter after casting a spell.

name: Mage of the Tides
benefit1: You can use Blue Tide.
benefit2: You gain Elemental Mastery: Water
benefit3: You gain the ability to walk on water and skate across its surface—every 10ft of water surface movement only costs 5ft of movement.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Blue Tide
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You create a 15ft radius zone of 1ft of water in a circle around yourself. This zone of water remains stationary at its original position, even after you move.

This zone lasts until the end of the encounter and automatically replenishes itself when Aqua Drill is used inside of it. You may have multiple of these zones active at once.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You create a 15ft radius zone of 1ft of water in a circle around yourself. This zone of water remains stationary at its original position, even after you move.

This zone lasts until the end of the encounter and automatically replenishes itself when Aqua Drill is used inside of it. You may have multiple of these zones active at once.

name: Mage's Journey
benefit1: You gain proficiency in the Channeling weapon group's weapons and gain Arcane Mastery. Mastery of an element allows you to use it in Channeling weapon attacks and use it to perform minor tricks relating to it.
benefit2: You gain +5 skill points in the Magic skill.
benefit3: You can use Sense Magic.

name: Magic Resistance
benefit1: You gain a +2 Anti-Mage bonus to your saves against Spells.

name: Maid Training
benefit1: You gain proficiency in staves (broom), small weapons, sets of missiles and light armor.
benefit2: You gain 5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Stealth, Roguecraft, Medicine, Common Knowledge, Linguistics, Appraise, History, Perception, Insight, Survival or Intimidation. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
benefit3: You may use Graceful Service.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Graceful Service
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You assist a target character, giving them 1 additional encounter conclusion action.

This uses up your own encounter conclusion action.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You assist a target character, giving them 1 additional encounter conclusion action.

This uses up your own encounter conclusion action.

name: Mana Sight
benefit1: You may use Mana Sight.
benefit2: One of your eyes becomes an Life Perception Eye. It may be used to make Mystic Eye type abilities of the appropriate type. An eye can only be a single mystic eye type.
benefit3: When making an attack with the Mystic Eye keyword that use the Life Perception eye, you ignore 1 point of guard.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Mana Sight
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You can assess the approximate mana percentage of a unit until the end of combat. (The DM should enable mana bar for the unit).
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You can assess the approximate mana percentage of a unit until the end of combat. (The DM should enable mana bar for the unit).

name: Martial Artist's Journey
benefit1: Your unarmed attack damage is treated as a One-Handed Weapon in which you are proficient.
benefit2: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Athletics, Perception, Insight, Medicine, Intimidation or a relevant Expert Knowledge skill (By DM approval). You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
benefit3: You can use Discipline.

name: Mass Index
benefit1: Your spells with the Gravity keyword deal extra damage to the target based on its size. They deal an additional 1 damage to large creatures and an additional 3 to Huge creatures.
benefit2: You may use Gravity Crush.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Gravity Crush
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You make a Light (1 AP), Heavy (2 AP) or Precise (2 AP) attack against the target that deals Physical (Crushing) damage.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You make a Light (1 AP), Heavy (2 AP) or Precise (2 AP) attack against the target that deals Physical (Crushing) damage.

name: Medic's Journey
benefit1: You gain proficiency in small weapons, set of missiles and light armor.
benefit2: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Medicine. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in this skill.
benefit3: You gain the benefits of First Response.

associatedAbilityRef.name: First Response
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: If you have a Medical Kit, you may use your encounter conclusion to give the Second Wind encounter conclusion effect to someone else. You heal for an additional 1d6 (+3 from your Demonblood Regeneration if you have it) when doing this.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: If you have a Medical Kit, you may use your encounter conclusion to give the Second Wind encounter conclusion effect to someone else. You heal for an additional 1d6 (+3 from your Demonblood Regeneration if you have it) when doing this.

name: Mending
benefit1: You may spend an EP to restore a Broken item. Some items (such as consumables) cannot be repaired (GM’s discretion).

name: Merchant Lord
benefit1: Procurement Limit increases to 3000 and you can procure materials that cost <= 3000 clim per ingot/block.
benefit2: You gain proficiency in Pistols.
benefit3: Upon mastering this class, the Procurement Limit increases to 4000 and you can procure materials that cost <= 4000 clim per ingot/block.

name: Mime's Journey
benefit1: You gain proficiency in 1 common and 1 specialized weapon
benefit2: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Art. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in this skill.
benefit3: As a mime, you’re not allowed to speak. If you speak during an encounter, you cannot use any Mime abilities until the end of the encounter.

name: Mission Control
benefit1: You gain proficiency in the Aetherie Sensory Station.
benefit2: You may use Mind Connection.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Mind Connection
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You physically touch up to five willing people, granting them Mind Connection—creating a mind link through you.  While linked, each creature can speak through telepathy to all other creatures linked to the same caster.  Mind Connection has unlimited duration but also reduces your maximum mana by 1, this effect lasts until you cancel Mind Connection.  Mind Connection has a maximum range of 500 ft—breaking this range doesn’t cancel the spell but interrupts the signal until within range again.

While Mind Connection lasts, you may spend 1 AP to transfer view through the eyes of one of the people connected with the mind link.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You physically touch up to five willing people, granting them Mind Connection—creating a mind link through you.  While linked, each creature can speak through telepathy to all other creatures linked to the same caster.  Mind Connection has unlimited duration but also reduces your maximum mana by 1, this effect lasts until you cancel Mind Connection.  Mind Connection has a maximum range of 500 ft—breaking this range doesn’t cancel the spell but interrupts the signal until within range again.

While Mind Connection lasts, you may spend 1 AP to transfer view through the eyes of one of the people connected with the mind link.

name: Mounted Basics
benefit1: You gain proficiency in Polearms, Lances, Light armor, Shields and one other common weapon group of your choice.
benefit2: You gain 5 skill points in Riding. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in these skills.
benefit3: You may use Spirit Jump.
benefit4: If you have mastered cavalier, your mount can withstand one lethal blow. If your mount would be reduced to 0 HP or below by an attack, it instead is reduced to 1 HP. Only works once per encounter.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Spirit Jump
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You and your mount jump up to 60ft vertically straight up into the air.  You can additionally move 5ft horizontally afterwards.  Unless your mount can fly, you automatically land straight down from your position at the end of your turn or for 0 AP, this falling movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity and you do not take fall damage from it.  You cannot make any non-teleport movement actions while in the air, but can still take other actions (such as attacking) while airborne.

You may trade height to move horizontally as part of the initial jump, moving 5ft horizontally as part of the jump in exchange for 5ft of height. You may move up to 40ft extra horizontally with this.

Instead of spending mana, you may instead spend a minute preparing this ability before activating it. When you do, this ability costs 0 mana.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You and your mount jump up to 60ft vertically straight up into the air.  You can additionally move 5ft horizontally afterwards.  Unless your mount can fly, you automatically land straight down from your position at the end of your turn or for 0 AP, this falling movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity and you do not take fall damage from it.  You cannot make any non-teleport movement actions while in the air, but can still take other actions (such as attacking) while airborne.

You may trade height to move horizontally as part of the initial jump, moving 5ft horizontally as part of the jump in exchange for 5ft of height. You may move up to 40ft extra horizontally with this.

Instead of spending mana, you may instead spend a minute preparing this ability before activating it. When you do, this ability costs 0 mana.

name: Natural Cycle
benefit1: At the start of your turn you may pay upkeep 1 mana for each Regrowth you have on an ally. If you do, you may extend its duration by 1 turn. You can choose this upkeep separately for each Regrowth.
benefit2: You may use Regrowth.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Regrowth
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You use nature's power to place Regrowth on an ally that lasts until the start of your next turn.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You use nature's power to place Regrowth on an ally that lasts until the start of your next turn.

name: Nightstalker
benefit1: You gain 60ft darkvision, allowing you to see perfectly in the dark even without a source of light. If you already have darkvision, the range of it improves by 60ft.

name: Ninja Style
benefit1: You gain proficiency in both Small Weapons and Sets of Missiles.
benefit2: You may use Ninja Run.

name: Ninja Techniques
benefit1: Your Ninja Run now lasts for the entire turn.
benefit2: You may use Genjutsu Kai

associatedAbilityRef.name: Genjutsu Kai
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml: You focus your internal energies and dispel any Illusions affecting you or an ally within 5ft of you.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText: You focus your internal energies and dispel any Illusions affecting you or an ally within 5ft of you.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementHtml: Target is under the effects of an Illusion.
associatedAbilityRef.requirementText: Target is under the effects of an Illusion.

name: On my terms
benefit1: While you are at 1 HP or higher, damage (including true damage) you take during your own turn cannot reduce you below 1 HP.
benefit2: When you roll a 1 on a skill check, something unexpected may happen (GM's discretion). This is usually bad, but it could also turn out to be a blessing in disguise.

name: Ore Hunter
benefit1: You can gather Tier 2 mining outcrops.
benefit2: You are an expert and tying knots. Using rope, you can secure yourself to objects and safely rappel down to investigate down cliffsides.

name: Overwhelming Critical
benefit1: When you roll a critical hit while making a Heavy attack with a Two-Handed weapon you may use Aftershock even if the attack didn’t miss. This use doesn’t count against the normal use limit of it and this effect still applies even if the critical hit was blocked or otherwise defended against.

name: Past Self
benefit1: You may use Past Self.
benefit2: Using the Delay encounter start action does not consume your encounter start action use and may use it multiple times.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Past Self
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You place a past self token at your current position that lasts until the end of the encounter. Your past self tokens are used for other abilities and cannot be attacked normally.

When you make a weapon attack, you may have the attack come from a past self token. After a past self token attacks, it becomes targetable by attacks and effects for 1 round as if you were in that position though you cannot be hit more than once by the same attack because of this.

Your past selves cannot be moved, willingly or forcefully.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You place a past self token at your current position that lasts until the end of the encounter. Your past self tokens are used for other abilities and cannot be attacked normally.

When you make a weapon attack, you may have the attack come from a past self token. After a past self token attacks, it becomes targetable by attacks and effects for 1 round as if you were in that position though you cannot be hit more than once by the same attack because of this.

Your past selves cannot be moved, willingly or forcefully.

name: Peddler of Wares
benefit1: Your in-combat burden capacity increases by 1.
benefit2: You gain proficiency in 1 common weapon group.
benefit3: You gain +5 skill points in Riding.
benefit4: Upon mastering this class, the Procurement Limit increases to 2000 and you can procure materials that cost <= 2000 clim per ingot/block.

name: Perfect Mimicry
benefit1: When given enough preparation time and enough knowledge about the subject, you are able to perfectly impersonate another person (GM’s discretion on what is ‘enough’). When disguised in this way, other people are unable to see through your disguise through normal means unless you act or say things that are strange enough to draw suspicions. This disguise is partially magical in nature, but cannot make up for obvious size or shape differences.

name: Phantom Thief
benefit1: You are able to disable and modify t2 and t3 bounded fields.
benefit2: You may use Stolen Skill.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Stolen Skill
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You may name one skill (such as perception, athletics) and force the target enemy to make a save against your Potency. On failure, you steal their skill points spent into the skill until the end of the encounter or for 1 minute. You gain their skill points they have in that skill to your skill while they lose all of theirs.

Only affects skill points spent, not any other bonuses from abilities or stats.

Can not be used on artisan and gathering skills.

You may only have 1 instance of Stolen Skill active at the same time, but you may choose to drop your current stolen one for 0 AP.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You may name one skill (such as perception, athletics) and force the target enemy to make a save against your Potency. On failure, you steal their skill points spent into the skill until the end of the encounter or for 1 minute. You gain their skill points they have in that skill to your skill while they lose all of theirs.

Only affects skill points spent, not any other bonuses from abilities or stats.

Can not be used on artisan and gathering skills.

You may only have 1 instance of Stolen Skill active at the same time, but you may choose to drop your current stolen one for 0 AP.

name: Pierrot's Journey
benefit1: You gain proficiency in small weapons, bludgeoning weapons and light armor.
benefit2: You gain +5 skill points to spend in Art. You can exchange any skill point for 2 expertise points, but must spend them in this skill.
benefit3: You gain 2 Damage Reduction against pies.

name: Puppet Grandmaster
benefit1: You can summon puppets under your control using Summon Piece. While these puppets follow movement rules as described in the abilities and are always Medium size, they can appear as whatever you wish, decided when you take this class.
benefit2: As an EP action, you may change the appearance of your pieces.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Summon Piece
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You summon a Pawn puppet under your control. It (and other puppets from this class) have HP equal to 20 + Focus, no evasion and no guard. These puppets are not living targets and are generally treated as structures rather than creatures.

They last until the end of the encounter.

Overcharge: You may spend extra mana to summon the following puppets instead:

1 Mana: Knight or Bishop

2 Mana: Rook

3 Mana: Queen
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You summon a Pawn puppet under your control. It (and other puppets from this class) have HP equal to 20 + Focus, no evasion and no guard. These puppets are not living targets and are generally treated as structures rather than creatures.

They last until the end of the encounter.

Overcharge: You may spend extra mana to summon the following puppets instead:

1 Mana: Knight or Bishop

2 Mana: Rook

3 Mana: Queen

name: Raise Undead
benefit1: You may use Raise Undead.

associatedAbilityRef.name: Raise Undead
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionHtml:
You raise the target corpse as a basic undead, such as a zombie. This zombie is treated as a non-heroic unit that you can command directly. You can have multiple zombies under your command, up to your Necromancer level + 1. However, the zombies act as a group under the same token rather than having an individual token. The group acts on your Initiative.

Each zombie has a single point of HP and immediately dies, but you only lose 1 zombie per attack the group takes, including AoE attacks that do not deal Holy Damage. AoE attacks that deal Holy damage remove a number of zombies equal to the damage taken. The zombie group has a focus and power of 3 + 1 for every 3 zombies, rounded down. They have an Agility of 2 and Toughness equal to 4. They have access to all basic actions, but otherwise have no special abilities. As a non-heroic entity, they only have access to a single RP. Because they are undead, they are immune to all mind-affecting effects.

A raised undead lasts until the end of the arc or until killed, after which the corpse cannot be raised again in any way.
associatedAbilityRef.descriptionText:
You raise the target corpse as a basic undead, such as a zombie. This zombie is treated as a non-heroic unit that you can command directly. You can have multiple zombies under your command, up to your Necromancer level + 1. However, the zombies act as a group under the same token rather than having an individual token. The group acts on your Initiative.
